learning_outcomes:
TRANN 1.  |  Major Programme:
Through
the course of their studies, students will have:
(1)  |  improved
their mastery of both English and Chinese languages and develop translation
and interpreting techniques to serve specific purposes;
(2)  |  acquired
major translation strategies and knowledge on key issues in translation
studies;
(3)  |  applied
appropriate linguistic and theoretical models in the production and
evaluation of translated texts;
(4)  |  developed
critical thinking and communication skills to engage in intellectual
discussions; and
(5)  |  reflected
on the dynamics of various elements (e.g. agency, language and ideology)
involved in translation activities, as well as the role of translators and
interpreters in the dissemination of knowledge, the construction of identity,
and the negotiation of power relations.
2.  |  Translation and
Chinese Language and Literature Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have acquired the
ability:
(1)  |  to
have a general understanding of Chinese language in everyday life and in
ancient time, and develop sustained interest in further pursuing some of the
linguistic issues; (K/V)
(2)  |  to
apply digital tools and databases in learning; (K/S)
(3)  |  to
show creativity and critical thinking in learning; (S)
(4)  |  to
demonstrate basic understanding of ancient texts and apply the methods of
reading them; (K/S)
(5)  |  to
gain the basic knowledge of literature and its development, and develop
sustained interest in further pursuing some of the literary issues; (K/V)
(6)  |  to
discuss the differences in literary texts, and apply literary concepts and
theories to analyse texts; (K/S)
(7)  |  to
express his or her ideas in a precise manner; (S)
(8)  |  to
identify grammatical properties of Chinese and English in a linguistically
informed manner; (K)
(9)  |  to
describe and compare the similarities and differences between Chinese and
English without prejudice, and be able to use this knowledge to produce high-
quality translations; (K/S)
(10)  |  to
synthesize the principles of comparative grammar and theorize about the
linguistic facts, and be able to apply this knowledge in translation
practice; (K)
(11)  |  to
develop sustained interest in further pursuing some of the linguistic issues
arisen from comparative grammar; (V)
(12)  |  to
understand and evaluate translations across a wide range of genres, including
literary, legal, business and media texts; (K/S)
(13)  |  to
produce effective translations, using appropriate strategies in a variety of
contexts; (K/S)
(14)  |  to
provide effective consecutive or simultaneous interpretation, using
appropriate strategies in a variety of contexts; (K/S)
(15)  |  to
be able to conduct independent critical reflection of translations in the
form of written and oral commentary; (K/S/V)
(16)  |  to
understand basic concepts of translation and apply these concepts to the
production and evaluation of translations; (K/V)
(17)  |  to
understand theoretical orientations in translation studies in their cultural
and historical contexts, and use appropriate theoretical models in the
production and evaluation of translations; (K/V)
(18)  |  to
understand the role of translators and interpreters in the dissemination of
knowledge, the construction of identity, and the negotiation of power
relations; and (K/V)
(19)  |  to
develop intercultural awareness, a lifelong interest in learning, and a love
for the richness of human expression. (K/V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude outcomes
3.  |  Translation
and Linguistics Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have:
(1)  |  attained
a body of knowledge about theories of language and language acquisition as
well as the associated methods of analysis; (K, S)
(2)  |  attained
high proficiency in English and Chinese for the purpose of effective
communication in academic discourse and the professional workplace, and at
least functional proficiency in Putonghua; (S)
(3)  |  the
capability to understand and evaluate translations across a wide range of
genres, including literary, legal, business and media texts; (K/S)
(4)  |  the
capability to produce effective translations and interpretations, using
appropriate strategies in a variety of contexts; (K/S)
(5)  |  attained
a sharpened awareness of the languages used in the local community through
their abilities in analyzing, primarily but not exclusively, English,
Cantonese, and Mandarin, in particular how these languages are structured and
acquired as a linguistic system, and how they function socio-linguistically
in the local community, the region and the world; (K)
(6)  |  developed
creative, analytical and critical thinking capabilities and a sufficient set
of research skills for work and postgraduate study; and (S/V)
(7)  |  developed
intercultural awareness, a lifelong interest in learning, and a love for the
richness of human expression, nurturing their personal, intellectual and professional
acumen. (K/V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude outcomes
4.  |  Minor
Programme:
Upon
completion of the Minor Programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired
knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a
suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects or
to pursue a related career.

Translation Translation Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 66 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Any 3 courses from: ANTH1020, ARTS1005, 1010, BMBL1001, CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902
or 1903, CURE1000 or 1400, 1110, ENGE1000, FAAS1900, HIST1000 or 1700
or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000, MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  TRAN1010, 1030, 2070,
2610  |  12
(b)  |  Any 2 capstone
courses from: TRAN4510,
4520, 4900 [a]  |  6
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  39
Students are required to complete 39 units of
elective courses from the following lists of courses, including at least
one course from each of Electives Cluster A and Electives Cluster B:
(a)  |  Electives
Cluster A: Translation Studies
TRAN2120, 3080, 3720, 4070, 4210
(b)  |  Electives
Cluster B: Interpreting
TRAN2140,
2150, 2240, 2250, 3240, 4050
(c)  |  Other Electives:
TRAN1120, 1130, 2020, 2050, 2110, 2130,
2840, 3170, 3180, 3270, 3280, 3620, 3800, 3810, 3820, 3830, 3844,
3845, 3850, 3860, 3870, 3880, 3890, 4800, 4840 and unselected courses
from Clusters A and B.
Total:  |  66
Explanatory Notes:
1. 2.  |  TRAN courses at
2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for
honours classification. Translation Major students are expected to be proficient in
both English and Chinese. Students may therefore wish to consider taking courses
that emphasize writing and speaking skills as part of their free electives,
on top of the major programme requirement. Listed below are courses for
reference: Course for writing skills in Chinese CHLL3403
Creative Writing Course for oral expression skills in Cantonese CHLT1302
Oral Communication Skills Courses for oral expression skills in Putonghua CLCP3703
Advanced Putonghua CLCP4713
Current Affairs and Discussion CLCP4723
Academic Presentation and Discussion Courses for writing skills in English ENGE3260
Creative Writing ELTU3413
Research Writing in the Humanities and Social Sciences Courses for oral expression skills in English ENGE2100
Research and Oral Reporting ELTU3502
Essentials of Public Speaking
[a]  |  For those
students who have chosen TRAN4900 Translation Thesis, the language used
in writing the thesis should be different from the target language of
their translation project for TRAN4510/4520 (e.g. if the student writes
the TRAN4900 thesis in English, s/he must take TRAN4510 Translation
Project: English-Chinese for Final Year Project).
Note on Double Major Linguistics-Translation:
1.  |  Translation Major student who
pursues a second Major in Linguistics can be exempted from taking LING3401 by taking TRAN2610. Units
earned can be counted towards both Major Programmes.

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  TRAN courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students may be required to participate in activities such as
field trips and study tours that foster their learning of linguistics. Students are
responsible for part of or all the costs incurred in relation to these
activities.
3.  |  Students are encouraged to learn a modern language or take
courses in a discipline relevant to a translation area in which the
student would like to specialize after graduation using their free
electives.
[a]  |  If the course is not taken to fulfill requirement 1, it may be
taken as an elective course for fulfillment of requirement 3(b)(iii).
[b]  |  The course not taken to fulfill requirement 2(b) may be taken as a free elective
course.

Major student who pursues a second Major in Linguistics can be exempted
from taking LING3401 by taking TRAN2610. Units earned can be counted
towards both Major Programmes.
Requirements for admission to the 2nd degree programme
1.  |  Students must receive first or upper
second honors in the first degree with a Major GPA of 3.3 or above.
2.  |  Students should normally have passed
all their undergraduate courses. Students who have any failure grades on
the first degree (i.e. Bachelor of Arts (Translation)) transcript will
not be permitted to proceed to the second degree (i.e. Juris Doctor) without
the consent of the JD Programme Director.
2nd Degree: Juris
Doctor (JD)
Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  15 or 18
LAWS6001,
6004, 6006[a], 6007, 6901 or 6902[b]
2.  |  Elective Courses:
Students are required to take at
least 54 or 57 units of courses from the following[c]: - Elective courses which are pre-requisites for subsequent
admission to the Postgraduate Certificate in Laws (PCLL) Programme: LAWS6011, 6013, 6015, 6017, 6018, 6019, 6020, 6021,
6022, 6023, 6024 - Other elective courses offered under the Juris Doctor
Programme - Elective courses offered under the Master of Laws Programmes  |  54 or 57
Total:  |  72
Other Requirements:
1.  |  Students must also fulfill the following requirements for
graduation:
(a)  |  Students must fulfill the Term
Assessment Requirement. A student who obtains a cumulative GPA below 2.0
in the preceding term will be put on academic probation. For details,
please refer to Clause 13.0 “Unsatisfactory Performance and
Discontinuation of Studies” of the General Regulations Governing
Postgraduate Studies from the Graduate School Homepage: https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/.
(b)  |  Students must have a cumulative
grade point average (GPA) of at least 2.0 in order to graduate.
Explanatory Note:
[a]  |  Students may take LAWS6089 to
substitute LAWS6006.
[b]  |  Students are required to take either
LAWS6901 (3-unit course) or LAWS6902 (6-unit course). The extra 3 units
completed in LAWS6902 will count towards the total units required for
graduation.
[c]  |  LAWS6012 and LAWS6014 will no longer
be pre-requisites for admission to the PCLL Programme with effect from 2021-22.
Students may still take the two courses as elective courses offered under
the JD Programme.

Minor Programme Title Translation
Minor Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 21 units of courses as follows,
with at least 6 units of courses at 3000 level or above:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  6
TRAN1010, 1030
2.  |  Elective
Courses:
Any 5 TRAN
courses, excluding TRAN1000, with at least 6 units of courses at 3000 or
above.  |  15
Total:  |  21
Explanatory note: 1. This Minor Programme is not applicable to
students who major in Translation, Translation and Chinese Language & Literature Double Major, Translation and Linguistics Double Major
Programmes and Bachelor of Laws/Bachelor of Arts (Translation) Double Degree
Option. 2. No more than 10 units of courses taken to
fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major programmes and other Minor
Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfil the requirement of this
Minor Programme.
